commit.message:
concept: add §4B Adaptive LLM Workflows — OpenClaw flexible LLM orchestration

Add comprehensive section on how OpenClaw with flexible LLM can adapt
workflows live:
- Agentic loop architecture (ask_llm_with_tools, ReAct-style)
- Provider tool-use support matrix (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
- Adaptive model selection based on task complexity
- 5 levels of workflow adaptation (L0-L5)
- Tool schema registry with bot.env configuration
- 3 live adaptation scenarios (voice CRM, mid-flow adjust, gemma4 audio)
- Static vs Adaptive comparison matrix
- Hybrid strategy: simple→static, complex→agentic
- 3 new decision points (#10-12)

Bumps concept doc to v1.2.

Update README Compatibility and Prerequisites sections (#9)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: apajon <7260230+apajon@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(workspace): automatic worktree + branch cleanup for dead agents

Reaper enhancements:
- Prunes doctor test branches (agent/_doctor_test_spawn_*)
- Tries multiple branch prefixes (agent/, task/, wave/) on cleanup
- Prunes remote tracking refs (git fetch --prune)
- New remove_worktree() public API for targeted cleanup
- ReapReport now includes branches_deleted count

Agent completion cleanup:
- spawn_monitor schedules worktree removal 5 min after agent exits
- Uses convergio-workspace::reaper::remove_worktree()
- Added convergio-workspace dependency to agent-runtime

GC endpoint:
- New POST /api/workspace/gc with configurable threshold_minutes
- Default: 60 min (vs 24h for scheduled reaper)
- MCP: cvg_workspace_gc tool (trusted ring)

Scheduled reaper unchanged: runs every 6h with 24h threshold.

Closes #673

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: rename sp-crossplane/CROSSPLANE_SP → sp-platform/PLATFORM_SP

The bootstrap service principal is not exclusively used for Crossplane —
it also authenticates Terraform runs, the ADO service connection, and the
WIF federated credential. Renaming to sp-platform (per-project) reflects
this broader role and mirrors the mi-{project}-platform naming convention.

Renames applied:
- Env vars:   BOOTSTRAP_SP_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/APP_OBJECT_ID → PLATFORM_SP_*
- TF var:     bootstrap_sp_app_object_id → platform_sp_app_object_id
- TF_VAR_:   TF_VAR_bootstrap_sp_app_object_id → TF_VAR_platform_sp_app_object_id
- Comments:   sp-crossplane / sp_crossplane → sp-platform / sp_platform

Files changed:
  scripts/simulate-pipeline.sh
  scripts/.env.smoketest
  scripts/.env.smoketest.template
  skeleton/terraform/variables.tf
  skeleton/terraform/terraform.tfvars
  skeleton/terraform/ado.tf
  skeleton/terraform/azure.tf
  skeleton/terraform/providers.tf
  skeleton/terraform/tests/test.tfvars
  skeleton/azure-pipelines.yml
  template.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add chunked generation for plugins-overview (per-plugin LLM calls)

The plugins-overview page exceeds GitHub Models' 16K token-per-request
limit when all plugin sources are sent in one prompt. This adds a
'chunked' strategy that:

- Generates each plugin section in its own LLM call (well under limit)
- Builds the bullet-list index deterministically in Python (no drift)
- Uses deterministic anchor IDs via slugify (index always matches sections)
- Only writes the page when all chunks succeed

Also updates the page spec schema to support strategy/chunk_source fields
and adds a chunk_section prompt template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix gotcha #33 — clarify dict-as-params fails at construction, not component access

immediately, so the AttributeError fires at model construction time —
not deferred until a component accesses self.params.R0 as originally stated.
Updated the error message to match the actual failure point.

commit.message:
Fix weather scraper selector to ignore new air quality section (#80)

* Fix weather scraper to scope selector to current-temps section

The Sun Peaks weather page now has multiple ul.list-temps sections
(current temperatures, air quality, and forecast). The unscoped
selector was matching all of them, returning 8 entries instead of 4
and breaking the daily tests since April 8.

Scope the CSS selector to div.current-temps to only match the
original 4 weather station entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests for current Sun Peaks page structure

- Add weather_current.html fixture capturing the new page layout
  with air quality section using ul.list-temps class
- Add test_parse_weather_html_ignores_air_quality to verify the
  parser only returns 4 temperature entries and excludes AQHI data
- Make today_icon and today_description assertions tolerant of the
  current-condition section being absent (removed off-season)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make live tests tolerant of off-season empty data

Snow conditions, wind speeds, and forecast sections may be absent
from the Sun Peaks weather page during off-season. Validate data
quality when present but don't fail when these sections are empty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Regenerate National Parks wallpapers with Opus-crafted prompts

commit.message:
Re-launch installer with -NoProfile to avoid import errors

When a user re-installs, their profile may already contain
Import-Module Cicada which fails before the module files are
copied. The installer now detects this and re-launches itself
with -NoProfile via an env-var guard. Also fixes duplicate
profile lines by removing the leading newline from Add-Content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: audit docs vs x130e code; update stale GPR spill and PV/PS claims

Audited SFN sources on x130e (mesa-26-debug at 568fc1deea0) against
canonical docs. Key findings:

- GPR_SPILL_DESIGN.md claimed 'DESIGN PHASE' / 'no spill fallback' but
  register_allocation_with_spill() is fully implemented and wired in at
  sfn_nir.cpp:1013. Updated status to PHASE 1 IMPLEMENTED.
- scheduler_model.md claimed spill is 'design target'. Updated to reflect
  Phase 1 implementation with Phase 2 targets listed.
- SFN_GAP_ANALYSIS.md had inconsistency: Law 1 (PV/PS) listed as PARTIAL
  in main table but FIXED in Appendix B. Updated main table to FIXED.
  Gap 2 (PV/PS scheduling-time optimization) marked RESOLVED.
- All other claims verified: KCACHE (Law 3), register constants,
  read-port validation (Law 4), Terakan SFN bridge, disk cache,
  dynamic state, terakan_sched.h Phase 0 contract.

New finding document: AUDIT_CODE_VS_DOCS_20260412.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pin devcontainer to Bookworm for docker-in-docker support

php:2-8 resolves to Debian Trixie (13/testing), which is not yet
supported by docker-in-docker's Moby packages. Pin to php:2-8.4-bookworm
(Debian 12/stable) where Docker packages are available.

fix: move Config sensors from CONFIG to DIAGNOSTIC entity category

Data Sync Status, Pi API Version, and Expected API Version are
read-only status/informational sensors. EntityCategory.CONFIG is
for user-configurable settings (text inputs, selects, numbers).
These sensors were showing as unavailable/greyed in HA's device
page because CONFIG entities are treated differently by the HA UI.

Changing to DIAGNOSTIC places them in the Diagnostics section,
which matches their read-only nature and fixes the unavailable state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- 第十九章: Hook Sidecar for custom VM lifecycle extensions

commit.message:
Fix: Load seeder directly using require_once to bypass autoloader issues

Some production environments have issues with PSR-4 autoloading for tenant seeders. Using require_once and direct file loading ensures compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(providers): remove hardcoded use_oidc from azuread provider

azuread provider had use_oidc = true hardcoded, which broke local SP auth
in simulate-pipeline.sh — with no ARM_OIDC_TOKEN available locally, the
provider could not authenticate.

Fix: remove use_oidc from the provider block entirely. Auth mode is now
controlled by environment variables only:
- Pipeline (WIF/OIDC): ARM_USE_OIDC=true + ARM_OIDC_TOKEN set by pipeline steps
- Local (SP):          ARM_CLIENT_ID + ARM_CLIENT_SECRET set by simulate-pipeline.sh

The azuread provider v3 reads ARM_USE_OIDC from env automatically, so the
pipeline behaviour is unchanged. Both providers (azurerm and azuread) now
use the same env-var-driven auth pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use related tickets (not just prerequisites) for dependency grouping

The dependency grouping was only using the Prerequisites section to find
ticket-to-ticket dependencies. Most dependencies are expressed in the
Related Tickets / Related Jira Tickets sections. Updated to:

- Use union-find to cluster tickets connected by either prerequisites
  or related-ticket references within the same directory
- Detect 'Related Jira Tickets' heading (prepared format) in addition
  to 'Related Tickets' (planned format)
- Fix field name parser to strip trailing colons from bold KV pairs
  (e.g. **Title:** was captured as 'Title:' not 'Title')
- Filter out -genlog.md generation log files from ticket scanning

commit.message:
Upgrade actions/create-github-app-token from @v1 to @v2 (#10)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Bumps `actions/create-github-app-token` from `@v1` to `@v2` in
`.github/workflows/publish.yml`.

`@v1` runs on Node.js 20, which GitHub Actions will deprecate on June 2,
2026 and remove entirely on September 16, 2026. This upgrade resolves
the deprecation warning seen in the failing publish run.

Resolves #7

commit.message:
Switch devcontainer to docker-in-docker for wp-env bind mounts

docker-outside-of-docker shares the host Docker socket, so bind mount
paths are resolved on the host — not inside the devcontainer. This
prevents wp-env from mounting the plugin directory into its WordPress
container, causing 'plugin could not be found' on activation.

docker-in-docker runs its own Docker daemon inside the devcontainer
where /workspaces/wp-document-revisions is a real path, so wp-env
bind mounts work correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
